examples: "This has never happened to me… never in my life," Markarian said of having three players injured at the same time.
The dual AGN of Markarian 739 is the second-closest known, both in terms of distance from one another and distance from Earth.
For decades, astronomers have known that the eastern nucleus of Markarian 739 contains a black hole that is actively accreting matter and generating prodigious energy.
The culprit is a staggering flare-up of Markarian 421, a "blazar" that hosts a supermassive black hole.
The name of Markarian 421 is cropping up in many talks at the American Physical Society meeting in Denver.
